summary: The canonical queue is behind append-only queue history: R-005 and R-006 are completed additive records but absent from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json. Detection does not authorize canonical mutation or prove peer execution.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical candidate source and test content before commit

privacy_boundary: Only public repository files, blob identities, commit identities, abstract state relations, and test outcomes were used. No raw private chat, health, pet-health, credential, contact, or uploaded-file content was published.
limits: The detector evaluates only supplied queue-update records; it does not enumerate the repository automatically.
Lag detection is not canonical adoption authorization.
A completed queue-update record is not evidence that a peer automation executed unless separate target-authored execution proof exists.
falsification_routes: Fetch the exact cited blobs and compare queue item identities and status.
Reject the finding if canonical state already contains byte-equivalent R-005 and R-006 records.
